category_title: Students with persistent challenging behavior referred for individualized behavior support
scenario_title: Independent work in math with escape-maintained behavior
minimal_context: The student is expected to complete a math assignment at their desk during an independent work routine.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for independent work. Please start the math problems on your desk.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Puts head down on desk) I don't want to do this.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to complete your math assignment now.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please return to your seat and finish your work.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Shouting and throwing the math materials across the room) I'm not doing it!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for independent work. Please start the math problems on your desk.

STUDENT: (Puts head down on desk) I don't want to do this.

TUTOR: You need to complete your math assignment now.

STUDENT: (Leaves seat without permission) No.

TUTOR: Please return to your seat and finish your work.

STUDENT: (Shouting and throwing the math materials across the room) I'm not doing it!

context.social_setting: naturally occurring instructional routines in elementary classrooms

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: putting head down on desk, leaving seat without permission, throwing objects, damaging instructional materials
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: social negative reinforcement (i.e., escape from tasks/activities)

behavior_patterns.internal_state: social negative reinforcement (i.e., escape from tasks/activities)
